methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Extropic is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis employs comparables from a universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductor companies, including same-stage private firms Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method uses the company's $50M total raised as a baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $100M valuation. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of four same-sector, same-stage comparables, yielding $1.5B. The peer set ranges from $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Extropic's revenue is not disclosed.

Triangulating these two methods produces a range of $85M to $1.7B, with a median of $800M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty about Extropic's position within its peer cohort. The stage-progression floor suggests downside protection relative to raised capital; the peer-median ceiling reflects sector-specific valuation momentum. Resolution hinges on product differentiation, customer traction, and capital efficiency relative to the named comparables.
